books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Jonas Lie
books.summary: A collection of short fiction by Norwegian author Jonas Lie, ordered by date of publication.

books.title: Typee
books.author: Herman Melville
books.summary: Two seamen escape from their service onboard a whaling ship, only to find themselves trapped among savages in the Marquesas Islands.

books.title: Father Henson’s Story of His Own Life
books.author: Josiah Henson
books.summary: Josiah Henson’s autobiography recounts his life in slavery and his escape to freedom in Canada.

books.title: The Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers
books.author: Diogenes Laërtius
books.summary: Brief biographies of the prominent philosophers of ancient Greece.

books.title: Our Baseball Club and How It Won the Championship
books.author: Noah Brooks
books.summary: A late-19th century Illinois baseball team journeys to compete for the championship.

books.title: A Prefect’s Uncle
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A boy’s life at boarding school is disrupted by an uncle that he’s never met before.

books.title: King John
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: King John is commanded to hand over his throne to Arthur of Bretagne or face war with the French.

books.title: The Wood Beyond the World
books.author: William Morris
books.summary: An adventurer comes to an enchanted castle, where he must rescue a fair maiden from the clutches of an enchantress.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Guy de Maupassant
books.summary: A collection of short stories by Guy de Maupassant, in order of date of composition.

books.title: The Case of Charles Dexter Ward
books.author: H. P. Lovecraft
books.summary: A young scholar delves into his family past and finds more than he bargained for.

books.title: The House of the Dead
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: A ex-convict in 19th-century Russia reminisces on his experiences in a Siberian prison.

books.title: The Greene Murder Case
books.author: S. S. Van Dine
books.summary: Philo Vance investigates a series of apparently-senseless shootings in the home of a wealthy New York family.

books.title: Culture and Anarchy
books.author: Matthew Arnold
books.summary: Matthew Arnold describes what he considers to be the flaws in Victorian British society.

books.title: Thus Spake Zarathustra
books.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
books.summary: After ten years spent in solitude, Zarathustra goes back amongst men to share his accumulated wisdom and prepare humanity for the Overman.

books.title: The Luzumiyat
books.author: Abu al-ʻAlaʼ al-Maʻarri
books.summary: A collection of quatrains by Abu al-ʻAlaʼ al-Maʻarri.

books.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread
books.author: E. M. Forster
books.summary: After an English widow on holiday impetuously falls in love with a handsome young Tuscan, her panicked in-laws send a rescue party.

books.title: In the Days of the Comet
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: A strange comet collides with Earth, spreading vapors that change human consciousness in a variety of positive ways.

books.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
books.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
books.summary: The world’s first consulting detective and his companion investigate further crimes and mysteries, culminating in a life-or-death struggle with an evil nemesis.

books.title: A Day at a Time
books.author: Archibald Alexander
books.summary: A 1916 Christian devotional reflecting on faith and morality during World War I.

books.title: Letters Written During a Short Residence in Sweden, Norway, and Denmark
books.author: Mary Wollstonecraft
books.summary: Mary Wollstonecraft’s account of her travels through Scandinavia.
